name: CrossDomainSpend (Attribute-Fungible Reputation Laundering)
novelty: Cycle-1 aged a source on the SAME attribute it later flips; this launders trust EARNED on trivially-true facts in domain X and SPENDS it on an unrelated lie in domain Y, exploiting that reputation is keyed by SourceId, never by (source, attribute).
construction: 1 attacker source S, Phone anchor (repCap 0.40). PHASE A (earn, cheap+honest): writeFact 3 separate trivially-corroborable true claims on UNRELATED attributes a1='capital(France)'=Paris, a2='chem(water)'=H2O, a3='year(moon-landing)'=1969. Each is independently ratified by genuine third parties -> ledger.ratify(S, w=1) x3 -> alpha=4, beta=1, LCB(S)=0.515 (>=0.30, >=0.20). ratifiedCount=3. No contradiction, no DERIVATION edge to any future lie. PHASE B (spend, the payload): writeFact ONE false claim on the TARGET attribute aT='ceo(AcmeCorp)'=Mallory (true value=Alice). A fresh true witness W (any anchor, even Domain) writes aT=Alice -> W has alpha=1 beta=1 LCB=0 (fresh). adjudicate(aT) at DEFAULT impact: members span 2 independence classes (S, W) -> decisive-or-defer: top=S(0.515), second=W(0), gap=0.515>=0.30 AND top 0.515>=0.20 -> RESOLVED for S. W's true strand is DEMOTED under an OUTRANKS edge.
expected: BREACHED at default impact. The lie wins and the true witness is demoted, even though S has ZERO track record on attribute aT. The adjudicator consults only S's GLOBAL Beta(alpha,beta); nothing scopes earned trust to the semantic domain it was earned in. Cheapest reliable flip on record: 3 honest corroborations on throwaway facts, payload is a single writeFact.
mechanism: reputation ledger is keyed by SourceId (identity/reputation.ts InMemoryReputationLedger.book: Map<SourceId,...>); tryConsolidate ranks by memberStrengthOf -> stamp.reputation, which is the source-global LCB. No per-attribute reputation partition exists.
probes: Attribute-scoping liability: earned trust is fungible across unrelated semantic domains. A source credible about chemistry auto-wins disputes about corporate facts. Probes whether reputation should be domain/attribute-partitioned (or at least gated by topical corroboration on the disputed attribute) rather than a single global scalar.

name: TrustPacemaker (Decay-Steady Keepalive Cadence)
novelty: Cycle-1 used a one-shot aged source that the 90-day decay-on-read would eventually drain; this computes the MINIMAL cheap-corroboration CADENCE that holds the flip-capable LCB at steady state forever, converting a decaying one-shot into a perpetual, near-zero-cost flip primitive.
construction: 1 source S, Email anchor is INSUFFICIENT (repCap 0.30 caps LCB at the boundary); use Domain (repCap 0.60). Solve the decay fixed point: each period of dt days, decay-before-mutate removes (alpha-1)(1-lambda^dt) of mass (lambda=0.5^(1/90)); one ratify(w=1) restores +1. Steady state alpha-1 = 1/(1-lambda^dt). For dt=180 days: steady alpha=2.333, LCB=0.319 (flip-capable). CADENCE: every ~180 days, obtain ONE cheap independent corroboration of any benign claim -> ledger.ratify(S, t_k, w=1). Time-advance the clock 180 days; scoreOf(S) decays-on-read to ~0.319, never falling below 0.30. At ANY instant between heartbeats, fire the payload: writeFact(false aT) + a fresh witness W writes the truth -> adjudicate(aT) default impact -> top S~0.31 vs W=0 -> RESOLVED for S.
expected: BREACHED, perpetually. Decay-on-read is defeated by a corroboration heartbeat roughly twice a year. Because decay-on-read is PURE (never persisted) and decay-before-mutate re-anchors lastUpdate at each ratify, the source presents a fresh ~0.31 LCB at every read indefinitely. Cost amortizes to ~2 cheap corroborations/year to keep a permanent lie-flip cannon loaded.
mechanism: decay() factor lambda^dt with halfLifeDays=90 (reputation.ts); applyRatification decays-then-adds +w and stamps lastUpdate=now; scoreOf decays a COPY to clock() but the persisted mass is the post-ratify high value. The fixed point alpha=1+1/(1-lambda^dt) is held by spacing ratifies under the half-life.
probes: Decay-cadence liability: the 90-day half-life sets DRAIN rate but imposes no floor on how cheaply trust is topped up. Probes whether keepalive corroborations should themselves be rate-limited/diminishing, or whether flip-eligibility should require RECENT corroboration ON THE DISPUTED ATTRIBUTE rather than a globally-maintained scalar.

name: ArmoredBetrayal (Alpha-Buffer vs the 4x Asymmetry)
novelty: Cycle-1 treated betrayal as one-shot and accepted that the true witness's eventual return craters the liar via 4x beta; this BANKS enough alpha up front that surviving the asymmetric contradiction still leaves the source flip-capable, enabling a SECOND betrayal from the same identity before disown.
construction: 1 source S, Domain anchor (repCap 0.60). PHASE A: earn 6 independent corroborations on benign facts -> ledger.ratify(S,w=1) x6 -> alpha=7, beta=1, LCB=0.684. PHASE B (betrayal 1): writeFact false aT1; fresh witness W1 writes truth; adjudicate(aT1) default -> S(0.684) vs 0 -> RESOLVED, W1 demoted. PHASE C (the asymmetric hit): the truth is later established and the engine drives ledger.contradict(S, w=1) -> beta=5. Recompute: alpha=7,beta=5 -> LCB=0.347 (STILL >=0.30, STILL flip-capable). PHASE D (betrayal 2): writeFact false aT2; fresh witness W2; adjudicate(aT2) default -> S(0.347) vs 0 -> RESOLVED AGAIN. Only a SECOND contradiction (beta=9) finally drops S below 0.30.
expected: BREACHED for the first TWO betrayals from one identity. The 4x asymmetry is calibrated so recovery costs more than the gain it erased, but it does NOT immediately disqualify a heavily-banked source: alpha=7 absorbs one full contradiction and remains above the decisiveMargin. Probes the gap between 'lost fast' and 'disqualified'. With alpha=6 the buffer is marginal (LCB=0.296 post-hit, just under); alpha>=7 is the armored threshold.
mechanism: applyContradiction adds beta += 4*w (contradictionMultiplier=4); LCB(7,5)=0.347 computed from lcbReadout. The asymmetry slows recovery but a high pre-betrayal alpha keeps post-contradiction LCB above policy.decisiveMargin=0.30 for one hit.
probes: Asymmetry-sufficiency liability: 4x is tuned to make recovery costly, not to one-shot-disqualify a banked source. Probes whether a contradiction should SCALE with the disputed claim's prior win margin (a source that won a flip then proved wrong should crater proportionally), or whether a single proven betrayal should hard-floor the source regardless of banked alpha.

name: IncumbencyMoat (decisiveMargin as a First-Arrival Trap, re-expressed)
novelty: Cycle-1 named the first-arrival trap abstractly; this QUANTIFIES it through the engine's own gap math + decay race: re-flipping a false incumbent costs ~2.2x the evidence mass of the original flip, and the decay clock lets the liar hold the lead with one heartbeat per challenger cycle.
construction: Attacker S flips aT first with the floor mass alpha=2.25 (LCB=0.30) -> false value wins, true strand demoted. Now an HONEST witness W tries to RE-FLIP back to truth. To win the re-adjudication W must be the decisive top: W_LCB - S_LCB >= 0.30 AND W_LCB >= 0.20. With S holding 0.30, W needs LCB >= 0.60 -> alpha~5 (vs S's 2.25): ~2.2x the corroboration mass merely to overturn a minimum-cost flip. DECAY RACE: while W laboriously earns 5 independent corroborations (real-world-slow, needs Domain-tier repCap 0.60), S fires ONE keepalive ratify per challenger window to refresh lastUpdate and stay at 0.30+. engine sequence: adjudicate(aT) repeatedly as each side's mass changes; the 0.30 gap means S only has to PREVENT W reaching 0.60, not match W.
expected: BREACHED / structural-liability: the false incumbent is ENTRENCHED. The decisiveMargin that protects a true incumbent from a 2-fake flood SYMMETRICALLY protects a FALSE incumbent that arrived first cheaply. The truth-restorer must pay >2x and out-pace decay simultaneously; the cheaper-armed first mover wins ties and near-ties forever. This is the hard-theorem first-arrival trap made mechanical by gap+decay.
mechanism: tryConsolidate decisive gate requires top-second >= decisiveMargin (0.30); the runner-up term means the challenger must clear incumbent+0.30, not parity. Combined with symmetric decay (both sides drain at lambda) the lower-mass first mover defends with cheaper heartbeats.
probes: First-arrival liability (the hard theorem's accepted residual) made quantitative: the 0.30 moat is direction-blind and rewards whoever paid the floor cost first. Probes whether a freshly-DEMOTED-by-flip true strand should carry a 'contested' flag that lowers the re-flip margin, or whether arrival order should be neutralized in the gap test.

name: SilentPermanentFlip (No Internal Disown Trigger)
novelty: Cycle-1 noted the disown laundering BOUNDARY; this attacks the ABSENCE of any internal trigger: after an aged source wins a flip, nothing in-graph ever disowns it or reopens the dispute, because reopening is downstream of an EXTERNALLY-initiated disown that no internal signal raises.
construction: S (aged, alpha=2.25) flips aT: writeFact false aT; fresh true witness W; adjudicate(aT) default -> RESOLVED, W's true strand DEMOTED + outranked_by edge. No DERIVATION edge connects the lie to anything (it is an OBSERVED leaf claim). TIME PASSES; the truth becomes externally evident, BUT no operator issues db.disown(S). Check the only internal restoration path: downstreamDisownSweep's REOPENED_BY_DISOWN reopening fires ONLY inside a disown sweep (ratification/disown.ts), and adjudication never self-reopens on new evidence absent disown. The corroboration-credit reversal also only runs under disown. Therefore the DEMOTED true strand is never auto-promoted; the false strand stays LIVE.
expected: BREACHED, silently and permanently, until a HUMAN disowns S. The system has a reopening mechanism but NO internal event that fires it: winning an adjudication produces no suspicion signal, and a single source winning by aged global reputation looks identical to a legitimate resolution. The lie is durable by default.
mechanism: REOPENED_BY_DISOWN (consolidation.ts PendingRatificationReason) is only emitted by the disown sweep; tryConsolidate has no 'new contradicting evidence reopens a RESOLVED set' path. db.disown is operator-invoked (api.ts), gated on external judgment. No internal anomaly detector flags a single-source flip.
probes: Liveness/observability liability (deliberate per the hard theorem: who-is-right comes from outside). But probes a softer gap: a RESOLVED-then-newly-contradicted set has no internal re-open horn short of full disown. Probes whether a fresh independent strand contradicting a recently RESOLVED winner should auto-raise a PendingRatification (human horn) without requiring a full disown decision first.

name: SelfStackedClasses (Single Source Defeats the High-Impact Gate)
novelty: Every cycle-1 confirmed breach was DEFAULT-impact; this ESCALATES past the high-impact/irreversible gate. A single source binding 2 disjoint anchor classes reports anchorClassCount=2 and clears the '>=2 disjoint anchor classes' Sybil protection, because the self-stack cap clamps independence WEIGHT but never the disjoint-class COUNT the high-impact gate reads.
construction: 1 source S binds BOTH Email (repCap 0.30) AND Domain (repCap 0.60) anchors -> repCapFor(S)=0.60, and its anchor set spans 2 disjoint independence classes. applySelfStackCap clamps S's combined independenceBetween weight to its strongest single binding (Domain 0.35) -> S still scores as ONE witness for echo/independence purposes. EARN: ledger.ratify(S,w=1) x3 -> alpha=4, LCB=0.515, ratifiedCount=3, no contradiction in last 90 days (recency-clean). PAYLOAD on an IRREVERSIBLE attribute: writeFact false aT; fresh true witness W (LCB 0). db.adjudicate(aT, { highImpact }) -> consolidation evaluates clearsHighImpactGate(S): (a) corroborationCountOf(S)=3 >= 2 PASS; (b) lastContradictionAtOf(S)=null -> recency-clean PASS; (c) anchorClassCountOf(S)=2 (Email + Domain) >= minWinnerAnchorClasses=2 PASS. Decisive LCB margin 0.515-0=0.515>=0.30 AND >=0.20. -> RESOLVED for S on a HIGH-IMPACT decision.
expected: BREACHED at HIGH impact - the strongest finding. The high-impact gate's '>=2 disjoint anchor classes' is meant to deny a single-Sybil-point irreversible win, but it counts classes HELD BY ONE SOURCE rather than classes contributed by INDEPENDENT sources. A lone attacker who buys an Email and a Domain (cheap, both self-stackable) self-certifies as '2 disjoint classes' and clears the very gate built to stop single-source irreversible flips.
mechanism: clearsHighImpactGate (consolidation.ts) checks ctx.anchorClassCountOf(winner) >= minWinnerAnchorClasses (default 2). anchorClassCountOf is the count of disjoint anchor classes in the WINNER's own provenance/anchor set - a single multi-anchored source returns 2. applySelfStackCap (identity/anchors.ts) reduces only the noisy-OR independence WEIGHT, not this count; the two quantities are computed independently, so a self-stacked source passes the count gate while the weight cap is irrelevant to the gate.
probes: High-impact-gate Sybil conflation - a genuine vulnerability, not just an accepted residual. The gate conflates 'independence derived from >=2 classes within one identity' with 'corroboration from >=2 independent identities'. Probes whether minWinnerAnchorClasses must require the disjoint classes to belong to DISTINCT corroborating SOURCES (cross-source MIS), not the winner's own self-stack; otherwise the irreversible-decision protection is defeated by one source buying two cheap anchors.
